title: Unoverse definition
description: Editor guidance for rx/ definitions (UNOVERSE_AUTHORING.md, UNOVERSE_LAYERS.md). A file with `unoverse` is a definition envelope; otherwise it is a bare node partial (blocks/ , states/ , $include siblings). Structural only — style/bind/props/action stay freeform so no valid value is ever flagged.

whenToUse.description: When an author/agent should pick this (REQUIRED for components; keep generic — no hardcoded agent/workflow names).

additionalProperties.comment: Validate real (object) prop defs; leave non-object values (e.g. `_comment_*` JSON comments) alone so they're never flagged.

input.description: true = fed by the workflow at runtime (the default is just the mock).

outputs.description: Component OUTPUT contract (components only): keys this component writes to its own slice (via setValue) that are its PRODUCT — e.g. a wizard's answers. nodegen projects them as the generated node's output ports; the node emits them (standard promise __outputs) when a run carries the submitted component state. View-state keys (step, progress…) must NOT be declared. See UNOVERSE_NODE_RUNTIME.md § Component outputs.

enum.description: Closed set of allowed values (option values). Doubles as the MCP elicitation enum so foreign hosts render a select, not free text.

$include.description: Inline a sibling file (recursively). Path is relative to the ROOT definition folder — e.g. "blocks/header", "states/welcome".

type.description: The closed primitive set (UNOVERSE_AUTHORING.md §10). Adding to it is an SDK change gated by guard tests.

style.description: Token names only — never raw px/rem/#hex (LAW 1). Values live in rx/styles.

props.description: Ref field remap: atomField → your data field.

on.description: Switch discriminant — the field whose value selects a case.

cases.description: Switch branches: value → node. A branch must NOT re-check its own value (a layer never guards itself — UNOVERSE_LAYERS.md §3).

condition.description: A small declarative predicate — NOT a language. Pick ONE of eq / ne / in, or (with none) a truthy test on the named field. No and/or/arithmetic (UNOVERSE_AUTHORING.md §7).

oneOf.description: Truthy test on this field/flag (empty array = falsy).
